title: The Co-Hosts
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:The Co-Hosts}}{{CSS|content= /* Font */ html .resizable-container, .page-content h1, .page-content h2, .page-content h3, .page-content h4, .page-content h5, .page-content h6 { font-family: Jost}}__NOTOC__{{VoidedTheme|mono}} {{Background|file=1130-2.gif}} {{CSS|content= .caius-text { font-family: IM Fell English; } }}{{CSS|content=@import url('https://fonts.cdnfonts.com/css/im-fell-english');}} [[File:Seal of Fun.png|center|link=|310px]] == Description == The '''Co-Hosts''' are an advisory group of around four to six [[Phenomenon 35-A|jovials]] who comprise [[Caius Taggert]]'s entourage, effectively fulfilling the entire rudimentary government system of [[Level Fun]]. They are some of the only [[Sanguine Festivus|infected]] individuals known to simultaneously be jovials and [[Sanguine Festivus|partygoers]]. The Party Host has alternatively referred to them as his "children", his "ministers" and his "pets"; while it was initially believed that they were "running dogs" utilised to preserve the illusion of freedom amongst the Partygoer Jamboree, through the recent procurement of more information regarding the matter, [[the Party Pooper]] has discovered that the Co-Hosts are indeed quite influential in the government of [[Level Fun]], working almost in tandem with rather than beneath Sir Taggert. The membership of the order has seen many iterations since the conception of Co-Hostship in the mid-1800s, ranging from the earliest, ''commedia dell'arte''-inspired retinue, to the modern, practical advisory council of manipulative and, to some extent, politically capable [[Phenomenon 35-A|jovials]]. The shift away from goofier characters to a more serious and practical approach has, according to [[Caius Taggert|Taggert]], been in accordance with the general rise of anti-[[Sanguine Festivus|partygoer]] [[groups]] and sentiments in [[the Backrooms]]. Interestingly, every generation of Co-Hosts seemed to have died together, giving rise to the theory that the Party Host actively orchestrates the replacement of each iteration with new, younger Co-Hosts. Each '''Co-Host''' is apparently hand-picked and infected,Co-Hosts are not selected from among [[Sanguine Festivus|the already infected]]. usually secretly, by [[Caius Taggert|the Party Host]] himself. Unlike other [[Sanguine Festivus|partygoers]], their minds are not [[The Afterparty|eclipsed]] upon infection, and instead they often find themselves wandering through [[Level List|levels]] in an uncoordinated and semi-lucid state, subconsciously wired to seek out the [[Level Fun/The Party Rooms|Party Rooms]], where Taggert will greet them warmly and integrate them into his council. Each Co-Host devises a "stage name" for themselves, whereby they are known among the wider public of the Wake, but the Party Host also gives them a new personal name in the spirit that they ultimately belong to him. It is presumed that the '''Co-Hosts''' themselves are fearful in some respect of [[Caius Taggert]], even if they show no outward signs of it. They indeed appear to treat him as merely an older brother-figure and occasionally mock him for his age, and a "suicide interview" with Co-Host Festus "Festhead" Zacharias has revealed his opinions of Taggert as a "nice, harmless old friend, if a little too uptight at times"; however, medical expert [[Asclepius Ang|Dr Asclepius Ang]] has expressed doubt that "a person, unless their amygdala is completely fried, is incapable of fearing, even just the tiniest bit, a man who has them hung up on puppet-strings, and whom they ''know'' has them hung up on puppet-strings, and whom they ''know'' they can never escape." == History == As with the history of [[Caius Taggert]] during the 19th and early 20th centuries, much of the information regarding the '''Co-Hosts''' during this time is largely unverifiable. === ''Commedianti'' (1843-1872) === The ''Commedianti'' were the first iteration of the '''Co-Hosts''', a group of four infected individuals based on popular archetypes from the Italian ''commedia dell'arte''. Their existence is slightly dubious, and only accounted for by [[Caius Taggert]] himself and in limited records of [[Sanguine Festivus|partygoer]] raids from the 1800s. According to such records, the ''Commedianti'' appeared only where Taggert appeared, and acted seemingly as extensions of his will rather than distinct individuals; their personalities were largely comedic and one-dimensional, perhaps suggesting that Taggert had not yet devised the nature of the Co-Hosts as [[Phenomenon 35-A|jovials]], or that the effects of Jovial Syndrome were originally much greater in similarity to those of commonplace [[Sanguine Festivus]]. The ''Commedianti'' met their end during an 1872 raid on the Wake Catholic Church, during which a desperate minister set the church building on fire, consuming both the [[Sanguine Festivus|raiders]] and the clergymen who were attempting to defend against them. {| class="wikitable" style="width:100%;" |+ The ''Commedianti'' |- ! style="width:25%;" | Stage Name ! style="width:25%;" | True Name ! style="width:50%;" | Description |- | Harlequin | Unknown | Based on ''Arlecchino'', Harlequin wore a checkered blue-and-red tablecloth with a ruff and carried a "[https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Slapstick slapstick]" as a weapon. According to records, he appears to have been extremely agile, able to dodge bullets. |- | Pantaloon | Unknown | Based on ''Pantalone'', Pantaloon wore a scarlet tablecloth and long curled shoes, typically walking slowly with a hunchback, but surprisingly quick when speed is required. He appears to have been the one responsible for collecting and bearing goods stolen during a raid. He apparently could not stand up by himself if he fell over backwards, leading some to believe that he was actually a very old individual. |- | Isabel | Unknown | Based on ''Isabella'', Isabel wore a purple-and-white tablecloth spread out near the bottom like a dress. Not much is recorded about her, but it is stated that her tablecloth did not cover her head, revealing her half-destroyed visage to all those around. |- | Scaramouch | Unknown | Based on ''Scaramuccia'', Scaramouch wore a striped black-and-white tablecloth with a ruff. He appears to have been given to taunting the Co-Hosts' victims as they perished, but rarely took a part in the killing itself, only barging in afterward to consume his share of the dead flesh. |} === Golden Generation (1876 - 1894) === So named in [[Caius Taggert]]'s (questionable) autobiography ''Your Gracious Host'', the Golden Generation (also known as the Circus Generation) was the second iteration of '''Co-Hosts''', formed shortly after the ''Commedianti'' perished in 1872. As with the previous generation, the Circus Co-Hosts are largely attested for by records of their raids on early [[groups]] such as the Wake Catholic Church and the Hellscapers, with additional insights into their alleged recruitment provided in ''Your Gracious Host''. Unlike the ''Commedianti'', these Co-Hosts had slightly more agency, apparently able to be sent out alone to perform dangerous tasks for the Party Host, although there is no evidence that they were anything more than Taggert's thralls at this stage. Records imply [[Phenomenon 35-A|jovial]] behaviour amongst the Golden Generation. The Golden Generation met their end when Calcifer McCoy the Firebreather, driven by pyromaniacal insanity, set the [[Level Fun/The Party Rooms|Party Rooms]] on fire, consuming the Co-Hosts in flames once again. {| class="wikitable" style="width:100%;" |+ The Golden Generation |- ! style="width:18%;" | Stage Name ! style="width:18%;" | Original Name ! style="width:18%;" | Caius's Name ! style="width:46%;" | Description |- | Acrobat | Favio Bianchi | Favonius Bianchi | An Italian trapeze artist who no-clipped into the Backrooms, Bianchi was supposedly chosen by Taggert for his agility. He had a habit of sneaking unnoticed onto ceilings during raids or assassination missions and dropping down onto enemies' heads, and seems from the accounts given to have had either unbreakable bones or an intensely high tolerance for pain. He was infected in 1876. |- | Farce | Farahilde Schäfer | Thalia Farahilde Schäfer | Schäfer was a Wakeborn of uncertain descent, an anarchist deemed insane by most even before her indoctrination by Taggert. She mostly dealt in explosives, and is known to have blown up various settlements during raids. Taggert has called her "one of the only Co-Hosts who truly deserved the role". She was infected in 1877. |- | Equestrian | Philip Chevalier | Philip Philip Chevalier | Chevalier was a young and homeless Frenchman who no-clipped into the Backrooms, whom Taggert stated he saw "no potential in", but selected him anyway because "he had a funny surname". Joining the Co-Hosts in 1878, Chevalier was notorious for riding a piked hobby horse into raids. |- | Firebreather | Calvin McCoy | Calcifer McCoy | McCoy was an elderly, Wakeborn English man and a revered Catholic priest. Upon his infection, Taggert warped his mind and turned him into a pyromaniac; he was often sent alone to important colonies so as to set them ablaze. He was the first of the Circus Co-Hosts, infected in 1876, and was often said to be the most dangerous of them all.

After he inadvertently killed the Golden Generation, Taggert claims to have kept his ashes in a jar, and sometimes consults the jar for "advice". Witnesses have stated that the jar speaks back to him, but researchers are fairly certain that this phenomenon is simply a result of Achospic Plane manipulation. |- | Beastwrangler | Orlando Öztürk | Bestiarius Orlando Öztürk | Öztürk was a Turkish man who no-clipped into the Backrooms, the last of the Circus Co-Hosts, infected in 1878. In [[the Frontrooms]] he had an affinity for animals, and had once worked in a travelling menagerie before being fired for liberating the animals out of "sympathy". After his integration into the Co-Hosts, Taggert apparently brought Öztürk an elephant,The elephant was allgedly a [[Phenomenon 9-A|thoughtform]] of Sir Taggert; what happened to the elephant after Öztürk's death is unclear. Researcher argue that the existence of the animal could be a hoax, and that records mentioning it were only the result of Akashic manipulation by Taggert. which he tamed and used often during raids, hence his stage name. |} === The Silver Generation (1899 - 1938) === The Silver Generation, or the Ensemble Generation, was the third iteration of the '''Co-Hosts''', as named in ''Your Gracious Host''. Owing to the increased establishment of major documentative [[groups]] — including the [[R.E.U.]], [[M.E.G.]] and [[Party Crashers|Anti-Jovials]] — there is more surviving data regarding these five individuals than the members of the ''Commedianti'' or the Golden Generation. The Silver Generation was more practical and down-to-earth than its predecessors, and it seems to have been in their time that the Co-Hosts became more active in the government of the [[Level Fun|Partygoer Jamboree]], rather than simply being the running dogs of [[Caius Taggert]]. This change coincided with the emergence of genuine threats to the [[Level Fun|Fun]] ranks à la the rapid establishment of the first Backrooms superpowers, which Sir Taggert claimed to have foreseen in his autobiography. In 1938, the Silver Generation was killed in a trap set for them on the borders of [[the Hub]]. The [[M.E.G.]] had foreseen an attack on [[Base Alpha|the Compass]] and placed pressure-triggered explosives beneath [[Level Fun]]'s hub-door, killing the Ensemble Co-Hosts instantly as they emerged. {| class="wikitable" style="width:100%;" |+ The Silver Generation |- ! style="width:18%;" | Stage Name ! style="width:18%;" | Original Name ! style="width:18%;" | Caius's Name ! style="width:46%;" | Description |- | Entertainer | Jessica Astoria | Joy Astoria | Astoria was a French woman who no-clipped into the Backrooms in 1895. She was the first of the Ensemble Co-Hosts, infected in 1899 by Taggert for her "potential in the field of humour". Originally a more solemn individual, Astoria's mind was warped by [[Sanguine Festivus|the virus]] to become utterly and unapologetically insane, much like Schäfer of the previous generation, although Astoria's specialty lay specifically in arson. She apparently told extremely dark jokes that the other Co-Hosts found amusing. |- | Conjuror | David Houdin | Hedylogus David Houdin | Houdin was an apparently American stage magician who no-clipped into the Backrooms in 1899, and was infected in 1902, the last of the Ensemble Co-Hosts. He was a subdued character as [[Sanguine Festivus|partygoers]] go, notable for having a strangely "hypnotic" voice that would muddle the minds of his victims, in front of whom he would then perform a few simple card tricks before picking a razor-edged Jack of Hearts from the deck, and slicing open their face. This performance was his preferred method of killing his victims, although he could reportedly also stab them with the card, or smother them with the long cape he wore behind his tablecloth. |- | Tragecomedian OR Doubledealer | Hermiona Sokolov | Hermaphroditus Sokolov | Sokolov was a Russian individual who began identifying as male upon no-clipping into the Backrooms in 1896. He was infected in 1900, and was known for his subtle manipulative skills. His mind was given to the skill of deception, and with some assistance to mask his appearance from [[Caius Taggert]] and his psychic powers, Sokolov was able to impersonate nearly anyone with even greater precision than some [[Entity 10|skin-stealers]]. His main task is summarised by Taggert as being "to lure unknowing victims into the clutches of the [[Sanguine Festivus]] virus". |- | Maestro | Benjamin Alley | Tim Ben Alley | Alley was an American luthier and musician who no-clipped into the Backrooms in 1896. Known for using the stringy portions of his victims' corpses to restring his fiddle, he was infected in 1901 by Taggert for his "musical talent", and is described as having a "rather rowdy" personality. Taggert states that Alley often wrote defamatory songs to degrade the memory of his victims in his free time. In 1927, Alley composed an experimental anthem for [[Level Fun]] which was never implemented, although translations of its lyrics into [[Party Greek]] have been discovered amongst Jamboree documents. |- | Candyman | Dulcie Douglas | Dulcie Douglas | Douglas was a Wakeborn British young woman who was infected by Taggert in 1899, notable for being the only Co-Host whom Taggert did not alter or replace the first name of. She was the most mysterious of the Ensemble Co-Hosts, with many records only briefly mentioning her as a "tall partygoer holding a lollipop"; nonetheless ''Your Gracious Host'' attests that she was a sweet but hot-tempered individual, who "provided some of the most vital ideas that led to the modern Jamboree", and that her lollipops were made of the frozen blood of her victims. |} === The Bronze Generation (1941 - 1968) === The Bronze Generation, also known as the Greatest Generation or the Warbler Generation, was the fourth iteration of the '''Co-Hosts''', comprising six [[Phenomenon 35-A|jovials]]. It was at this stage that [[Caius Taggert|the Party Host]] began to allow the Co-Hosts more agency, a large turning point in the system of Co-Hostship being, according to him, his allowing of the Co-Hosts to keep their own personality and opinions prior to infection, only using non-psychic means to manipulate them. In this way, Taggert claims, the Co-Hosts first became an advisory council to the Party Host rather than a mere entourage. With the rise of the [[Party Crashers]] and other enemy [[groups]], the Co-Hosts prompted the Jamboree to begin instigating more precise and subtle infections where violent and unplanned raids would previously have been orchestrated; the most infamous of these epidemics is undoubtedly the 1960s' First Crash in [[Level 52]]. The Bronze Generation was singlehandedly killed off by [[The Party Pooper|Thomas Jefferson Wilson]] in 1968, who flew into a rage at their cannibalisation of his friends. {| class="wikitable" style="width:100%;" |+ The Bronze Generation |- ! style="width:18%;" | Stage Name ! style="width:18%;" | Original Name ! style="width:18%;" | Caius's Name ! style="width:46%;" | Description |- | Skylark | Bethany Nickelsworth | Skyler Bethany Nickelsworth | Nickelsworth was a British young woman who no-clipped into the Backrooms in 1949, and was infected and integrated into the Co-Hosts in 1950. [[Caius Taggert|Taggert]] claims she was a practical, intelligent and strong-willed character, but also sprightly and "able to have a good laugh", being overall an individual who suited his purposes at the time almost perfectly. He claims that out of all the Co-Hosts he has ever had, Nickelsworth was perhaps his favourite. She also had a close bond with [[The Party Creator|Thaumas Blake]], possibly maintaining a romantic relationship with him, and he (as [[the Party Creator]]) named the Nickelsworth Memorial Arcade in [[CJNF|Discothèque]], [[New Fun]] after her. |- | Toymaker | Oliver Wang | Theodore Oliver Wang | Wang was a Wakeborn Chinese man who worked for the [[Tezo's Art Supplies]] for some time, before being infected in 1941, the first of the Warbler Co-Hosts. True to his sobriquet, his specialty was in crafting toys which would appear innocent on the surface, but would often conceal dangerous weaponry within them, such as exploding Rubik's cubes or playing cards with sharp metal edges (the latter were inspired by H. D. Houdin's paraphernalia). [[Caius Taggert|Taggert]] has called Wang a "bundle of joy" and states that he "truly spices up all of our lives". Thaumas Blake, as [[the Party Creator]], has called him "an annoying little prick". |- | Wonderworker | N/A | [[The Party Creator|Thaumas Blake]] | Blake is a Wakeborn British man who wandered into [[Level Fun]] at a young age, separated from his parents, and was raised by [[Caius Taggert]], eventually being introduced to the entourage of Co-Hosts in 1957. According to Taggert, he was one of the greatest Co-Hosts, a brilliant and reliable advisor who displayed surprising pragmatism for a [[Sanguine Festivus|partygoer]]. Infected in 1946, Blake is the youngest and last of the Warbler Co-Hosts, but his excelling qualities quickly brought him to the head of the order. [[The Party Creator]] claims to be Blake, resurrected after his death at the hands of [[the Party Pooper]] by a "sympathetic god". |- | Colourman | Viridian Faragó | Himerus Viridian Faragó | Faragó was a Hungarian man who no-clipped into the Backrooms in 1937, infected and integrated into the Co-Hosts in 1942. He previously worked as a designer for the [[M.E.G.]] and was responsible for several murals around [[Base Alpha]] which have since been covered over. Street-smart and quick-minded, Faragó turned his craft to architecture a few short months before his infection. He is responsible for the unstable structure of the Citadel of Fun in the [[Level Fun/The Party Rooms|Party Rooms]], the seat of the Party Host's power in [[Level Fun]]. In 1960, he was shot in both ears by [[Party Crashers]] and became deaf. |- | Jack-in-the-Box | João González | Jack González | González was a Wakeborn youth of apparently Hispanic descent who wandered into [[Level Fun]] in 1945, and was subsequently infected and brought into the Co-Hosts. He was a young man of small stature and great agility, with those whom he chose to kill or infect supposedly unaware of his presence until he appeared suddenly before them, hence his rather appropriate stage name. [[Caius Taggert|Taggert]] mentions his fluctuating personality, and how he would often turn silent during meetings for extended periods of time, only to inexplicably blurt out an opinion or criticism with unprecedented enthusiasm. |- | Swanmaiden OR Ballerina | Olivia Seeley | Olorina Seeley | Seeley was an American young woman who no-clipped into the Backrooms in 1939, and was infected and brought into the Warbler Co-Hosts in 1952. Apparently affected with HSAN5, Seeley was unable to feel sensations such as temperature and pain, committing such treacherous feats as a result that she broke multiple bones and twisted her body into an uncannily contorted position during her time as a Co-Host. While her official stage name was ''Swanmaiden'', in ''Your Gracious Host'', [[Caius Taggert|Taggert]] displays a habit of consistently referring to her as "[[Entity 71|Ballerina]]"; this is likely due to her reported tendency to walk on her toes because of her warped posture. The Olo-Ring nightclub in [[CJNF|Discothèque]], [[New Fun]] was named after her. |} === The Pewter Generation (2004 - Present) === The Pewter Generation, also called the "Strawberry Generation", is the fifth iteration of the '''Co-Hosts''', as defined in ''Your Gracious Host''. It comprises five [[Phenomenon 35-A|jovial]] members, and is the most widely known version of [[Caius Taggert]]'s entourage. The Pewter Co-Hosts, according to Taggert's autobiography, were given the greatest range of personal freedom and agency of the five generations, "almost ''too'' much agency, in fact, considering their rebellious and impertinent tendencies". This generation is responsible for many of the decisions and advances made during the Euphoric Wars and [[the Flicker]], as well as against groups such as the [[Party Crashers]], the A.E.G. and (to some extent) the [[CJNF|Celebrants' Jamboree]]. Taggert claims that "alongside the Greatest Generation, the Strawberries have had the largest effect of all the Co-Hosts on the shaping of modern Fun".[[Caius Taggert|Sir Taggert]] claims the Pewter Co-Hosts "taught [him] how to use a computer", although whether this is metaphorical (referring to them having a large and positive impact on his governance of the [[Level Fun|Partygoer Jamboree]]), or if they literally educated him in regards to modern technology, remains unclear. The three decade long interval between the Bronze and Pewter Generations remains unexplained. Some believe that [[Caius Taggert]], having grown overly attached to the Co-Hosts of the Bronze Generation (especially [[The Party Creator|Thaumas Blake]], whom he raised as a foster father figure), was shocked by their sudden deaths and felt true grief for the first time in his long life, prompting him to step away from relationships as a whole for a time. However, there are a good number of psychologists who find this theory rather unlikely, while some instead propose that the Party Host was devoting this time to [[Maria|his partnership with genius inventor Tobias Holdings]], and found that an entourage of Co-Hosts would only hinder him in this. The Director of the [[Party Crashers]] has stated that "it is wiser to leave the mystery of the Party Host's mind, well, a mystery." {| class="wikitable" style="width:100%;" |+ The Pewter Generation |- ! style="width:18%;" | Stage Name ! style="width:18%;" | Original Name ! style="width:18%;" | Caius's Name ! style="width:46%;" | Description |- | Sawbones | Europa Kronenscheldt | Euphoria Kronenscheldt | During her life in [[the Frontrooms]], Kronenscheldt was a German-American surgeon and infectiologist, previously a paramedic, practising in the United States. She no-clipped into the Backrooms in 1996, and was subsequently infected in 1998 after a short career practising for the [[Backrooms Red Cross]], marked by [[Caius Taggert]] for her intelligence, practicality and ability to "have a good laugh". According to ''Your Gracious Host'', Kronenscheldt reminded Taggert of S. B. Nickelsworth, his personal favourite Co-Host. Kronenscheldt continues a medical practice in [[Level Fun]] mostly "out of habit", tending to wounded [[Sanguine Festivus|partygoers]] even though neither they nor anyone else cares whether they live or die; her medical procedures are often impeded by Taggert and the other Co-Hosts "for fun", much to her chagrin.

Kronenscheldt's left eye was shot out by [[Party Crashers]] during the Euphoric Wars, leaving her half-blind. She reportedly maintains the best relationship with [[Caius Taggert]] of the five Pewter Co-Hosts, being his most trusted advisor and his main strategist during the Euphoric Wars. He commends her for not fearing to contradict and to provide her own opinions on his designs, stating that her ideas have often opened him up to a way of thinking he had never before considered, although "in the end, my original conceptions are still collectively better than what little I accept of her additions". She was already acquainted with Sir Taggert for six years before becoming the first of the Pewter Co-Hosts in 2004. |- | Drunkard | Orion Weintraub | Bacchus Orion Weintraub | Weintraub is a German individual who immigrated to the United States during his early life, alongside his parents and younger sister. When he was around sixteen years old, he experienced a traumatic incident which would cause him to become haemophobic for the rest of his life; it has been hypothesised that this incident was the murder of his parents and sister, but neither Weintraub nor [[Caius Taggert]] have directly confirmed this popular speculation. In 2003, he no-clipped into the Backrooms, and was subsequently infected and integrated into the Co-Hosts in 2005, after a while of solitary wandering across the [[:Category:The Main Nine|Main Nine]]. Taggert apparently selected him because he "needed someone hardened to pain and killing, someone who can handle murder on a daily basis without my warping of his mind; but at the same time I needed someone who is human and understands human nature at an intimate level: ''know thy enemy'', as Sun Tzu says in ''The Art of War''."

Weintraub is apparently the most collected and staid of the Pewter Co-Hosts, largely owing to his intense haemophobia, a trait he has maintained even after a partial descent into insanity from the [[Sanguine Festivus]] virus. Horrified rather than thrilled by the [[Sanguine Festivus|partygoers]]' blood-soaked parties and violent tendencies, Weintraub refuses to participate in any gruesome and gory undertakings on behalf of the Jamboree, instead taking a more "backseat" role in planning and strategy. He only eats blood-free flesh, and only drinks wine;Weintraub claims to grow the very grapevines whence the wine he drinks on a daily basis descends, but researchers think it more likely that the wine is a [[Phenomenon 9-A|thoughtform]] or simply stolen from other [[groups]] during raids. he reasons that he is proving a point in doing this: he is still "the most sober of the Co-Hosts" despite being in a constant state of intoxication, precisely because he has not spiralled down a path of violence and madness as they have. The other Co-Hosts appear to consider him a pusillanimous and "boring" individual due to his haemophobia. According to ''Your Gracious Host'', Weintraub learnt to read braille and use [[Backrooms Sign Language|sign language]] despite being neither blind nor deaf, supposedly as a "precaution" should he ever become sensorily impaired. |- | Festhead | Fletcher Zacharias | Festus Zacharias | Zacharias is a British Wakeborn mechanical engineer who joined the [[The Kauer Research Organization|Kauer Research Organisation]] and learnt engineering as a side commitment, subsequently being infected and integrated into the Co-Hosts in 2007. ''Your Gracious Host'' states that [[Caius Taggert|Taggert]] selected Zacharias for his practical skills and calculating mind, citing his previous experience in weaponry engineering (firearms, explosives, etc.) to be one of the prime reasons for this choice. However, the Party Host has also stated that he often finds Zacharias to be "not nearly insane enough for my liking", expressing a desire that he would "loosen up" and become as maniacal as Golden Generation Co-Hosts Schäfer and McCoy.

Zacharias was shot in the ear by [[Party Crashers]] during the Euphoric Wars. He is responsible for many of the shrewd devices used by the [[Level Fun|Partygoer Jamboree]] in the aforementioned [[:Category:Conflict|conflict]] and more, his contrivances including airguns, makeshift war-machines, false [[Phenomenon 18|safe places]] and "oobleck pits" to counter [[Object 5|Greek fire]]. He has been known to work closely with E. Kronenscheldt, [[Caius Taggert]]'s main strategist and "lieutenant" during the Euphoric Wars, who often provided early conceptions of and ideas for Zacharias's designs. He apparently maintains friendly relationships with the rest of the Co-Hosts due to his outgoing personality,Zacharias does rather dislike B. Weintraub, nonetheless. although he has sometimes stated that they seem to forget he is present at times, and has defined himself as "the middle child" of the Pewter Co-Hosts. Sir Taggert claims Zacharias reminds him of T. O. Wang the Toymaker. |- | Fleshmonger | Belle Evenstar | Melody Belle Evenstar | Evenstar is a Wakeborn individual of apparently European descent, who was affiliated with the [[Backroom Colonists]] before being infected and integrated into the Co-Hosts in 2005. [[Caius Taggert]] states in ''Your Gracious Host'' that he selected her for her eccentricity and ability to think "well outside the box", being one of his most idiosyncratic and unhinged Co-Hosts since the likes of F. Schäfer, of the Golden Generation. Evenstar's field of proficiency lies in the culinary sphere, and she was allegedly well-known amongst the Colonists for her inventive recipes, described using terms ranging from "innovative" and "creative" to "disturbing in the most visceral manner possible". She is one of the only Co-Hosts who, rather than being drawn to [[Level Fun]] by Taggert's psychic manipulation, actively departed from her group to seek out a more interesting and unconventional life.

Evenstar is notoriously blind, having lost both of her eyes in the early stages of her [[Sanguine Festivus|infection]]; she refuses to learn braille, but is apparently surprisingly adept at relying on her other senses to navigate her daily life. According to Sir Taggert, she also presents with an unhealthy aversion to "spoilers", often refusing to listen to others with the reasoning that they "might spoil something"; these "spoilers" can range from important offensive strategies against enemy [[groups]] to decisions relating to the governance of [[Level Fun]], and Evenstar's unwillingness to heed any of these makes her (in F. Zacharias's words) the most useless of the Pewter Co-Hosts. She enjoys toying with the other Co-Hosts, such as stealing and killing patients from Kronenscheldt's practice to cook and eat, or appearing to Weintraub covered in blood. In eyewitness accounts, Evenstar is often observed carrying a dented frying pan, which she may use as a weapon. |- | Sweetcakes | Helena Elpis | Hedone Elpis | There is not much information present in regards to Hedone Elpis. She is likely to be a young woman of eastern European descent, and is known to have been infected and integrated into the Co-Hosts during 2011 — thus being the last of the Pewter Generation — yet beyond this, even [[Caius Taggert]] states in his autobiography that he is "unsure where exactly she came from". In ''Your Gracious Host'', it is stated that Elpis has been affected with a congenital inability to identify emotion since birth, likely referring to the medical condition of alexithymia; after being brought into the Pewter Generation, Taggert's propagation of the value of happiness led her to eventually begin interpreting all of her emotions as joy. Described by Sir Taggert as innocent and sweet-looking, she is apparently often tasked to lure unsuspecting wanderers into the clutches of the [[Sanguine Festivus]] virus; Taggert has called her "a nigh-perfect reincarnation of Hermaphroditus", referring to H. Sokolov of the Silver Generation and the similar role he played in comparison to Elpis. |}
Twelve were dead, thirty infected, sixty-three wounded that day. The partygoers ransacked everything. Only half of the base's population managed to get away. Barely. The rest stayed behind to fight. First was that old man in his seventies who's lived through a thousand bloodbaths. Then his nineteen year old daughter, tears streaming down her contorted face. Then the three men in their forties who'd been trampled underfoot by the oncoming stampede. Then the young men who wore hoods together and laughed about it. They weren't laughing anymore. And then there were none. Blood, blood, everywhere. Mangled corpses and pale, lifeless faces. Figures in dirty yellow rushing past me. Flames crackling in my ears. Blood, blood, everywhere. I simply stood there watching the whole thing unfold before me. Just standing there with blank eyes, as always. A figure strode up to me. Tall with a sack for a face. Blood, blood, all over Him. "Well, well, well," He says. "What do we have here?"
Ⓗ
What is life without the thrill of experiencing something for the first time? That's why I got into cooking. You can make something new, you can make a thousand things new, you can experiment and you can tinker. You can make new recipes. You can use new ingredients. You cannot be chastised for it. You can make something that is yours, because it is not anybody else's. You can make something that is new, because it's never been done before. I am not like the others. He never appeared to me. I looked for something new. Something fresh. Something exhilarating. Something ''greater''. And I found Him. No spoilers, that's my mantra. Because life doesn't deserve to be spoiled.
Ⓜ
When I worked for Kauer, I learned some interesting things. This world only exists as a product of our minds. This world is built on our imaginations, our fears and our hopes, our dreams and our dread. This world is built solely on the the human consciousness. Without the human mind, the Backrooms wouldn't exist. I always thought, What if you could use this to your advantage? If the world is built on our minds, Why can't we tear it down and rebuild it just how we want? Then I met someone who was doing just that. Someone who was tearing the world down and rebuilding it just how he wanted. That someone was Him. The world is yours. Don't waste it.
Ⓕ
Twenty-five years ago, my world ended. I came home from school. I thought I would find them as they always were. My mother setting the table My father reading the newspaper or listening to the wireless My sister doing her schoolwork They weren't. My mother wasn't My father wasn't My sister wasn't The house was covered in blood. Blood, blood, everywhere. I saw who did it. His face was half in shadow. He was tall. He was gloved. He was wearing something over his face. He leapt out of the window. I never saw Him again. The world is a cruel lie. Happiness is a cruel lie. There is only the hard truth that the world is too afraid to face. So why not delude yourself, if for a little while, that you are happy?
Ⓑ
When I first met Him, He asked me if I would like to be his personal medical advisor. At the time, I thought, ''Why not?'' My life was getting boring anyway. I needed something to spice it up. The first few years in Level Fun were fine. He was in perfectly good health despite being over three hundred years old, so my days were mostly spent chatting with Him rather than giving Him rigorous surgeries and whatnot. I'd say we became fairly good friends. After a while I started to join Him on raids and escapades. Good times, as always. But as we conquered the next M.E.G. base and then the next and the next, my life began to lose its meaning. I wanted to be a doctor again. I restarted my practice in Level Fun. It didn't feel the same. Nobody cared about these patients, cared that they were dying. Their families didn't care that they were dying; their families probably thought them dead a long time ago. I didn't care that they were dying. Not even they themselves cared that they were dying. But still I continued to admit patients. I don't know why. I always told Him I was doing it for the ''fun'' of the job, whenever he asked me why I couldn't just let a few patients die. Really, I think it was because I desperately needed some meaning in my life. I needed to feel like I was doing something important, something structured. Something beyond the inconsequential randomness of His mind. Something that could make me feel ''human'' again. Even if, ultimately, I was doomed to mindless beasthood like all others under His will. ... I can hear carols being sung outside. Badly. I'm pushing thirty milligrams of almond water into a patient's veins. The patient relaxes. I inject a sedative. He appears at the door. "Aren't you joining the festivities, Euphoria?" "No thanks." "That's always your answer." "That's always your question." He laughs. "Well, you do you, I suppose... It'll be a Festivus for the rest of us." I smile. Not because I'm happy, but because I've forgotten how to stop smiling. That's the cruel, twisted truth behind His gift, isn't it? "Happy Festivus, Caius."
Ⓔ
